tests.name: Learning layer exists (behavior verified)
tests.evidence: Initial: ask, After 1 approval: ask. APPROVAL_THRESHOLD=3 found: true. Learning layer verified via behavior.

tests.name: Learning layer with threshold check
tests.evidence: After 2 approvals, action: ask (expected: ask). Threshold-based learning found: true

tests.name: Auto-approve promotion from 'ask' to 'allow'
tests.evidence: After 3 approvals, action: allow, reason: learned pattern (3 approvals). Promotion logic found: true

tests.name: Threshold of 3+ for auto-approve
tests.evidence: At 2 approvals: ask, At 3 approvals: allow. 3+ threshold found: true

tests.name: Dangerous command detection - rm -rf
tests.evidence: rm -rf / blocked: true, reason: dangerous pattern

tests.name: Dangerous command detection - dd
tests.evidence: dd blocked: true, reason: dangerous pattern

tests.name: Dangerous command detection - sudo rm
tests.evidence: sudo rm blocked: true, reason: dangerous pattern

tests.name: /permissions reset clears learned patterns
tests.evidence: Before reset: allow, After reset: ask (expected: ask). Reset logic found: true

tests.name: Learned patterns written to permissions.json
tests.evidence: permissions.json exists: true, Contains learnedPatterns: true

tests.name: Reproducible: ask x3 -> allow on 4th
tests.evidence: Cycle: ask -> ask -> ask -> allow, Expected: ask -> ask -> ask -> allow. Reproducible: true

verdict: Implementation is REAL and WORKING. The learning layer properly tracks approvals via behavior (approvalCount is internal but works correctly), auto-approves after 3+ approvals, blocks dangerous commands, persists to permissions.json, and supports /permissions reset.
